calibration.calibration_note: With hazard_harm=0.02, avg episode ~182 steps (was ~18 with 0.5). Actual episode time higher.

items.title: SD-010 Harm Stream Fixed — normalized labels, no sigmoid, stratified calibration
items.description: Rewrite of EXQ-056a. Fixes: (1) Sigmoid removed from harm_eval_z_harm_head; (2) normalized label harm_obs[12] used for both training and eval; (3) event-stratified replay buffer ensures balanced none/approach/contact in each training batch. PASS: C1 r_z_harm>0.30, C2 r_z_harm>r_z_world+0.10, C3 z_harm_variance_across_event_types>0.01, C4 n_contact>=30.

items.title: SD-010/SD-003 Attribution Fixed — normalized labels, no sigmoid, stratified calibration
items.description: Rewrite of EXQ-058a. Same three fixes as EXQ-056c. Three-phase: terrain 400 → E3 calibration 200 (stratified) → eval 100. PASS: C1 causal_sig_approach>0.001, C2 calibration_gap>0.05, C3 mean_harm_eval_none<0.2, C4 causal_sig_contact>causal_sig_approach, C5 n_approach_eval>=30.

items.title: SD-010/MECH-102 Advantage Fixed — normalized labels, no sigmoid, stratified calibration
items.description: Rewrite of EXQ-059a. Same three fixes as EXQ-056c. 700 warmup eps to get n_contact>=30. Ethical policy uses argmin harm_eval_z_harm(harm_enc(harm_bridge(E2(z,a)))). PASS: C1 advantage_sig_contact>advantage_sig_none, C2 advantage_sig_contact>0.001, C3 world_forward_r2>0.05, C4 n_contact>=30.

items.title: ARC-016 Relative Threshold — calibrated from training baseline variance
items.description: Rewrite of EXQ-018. Replaces absolute commit_threshold with relative: threshold = 2.0 × training_baseline_variance, calibrated from final 100 training episodes (sleep recalibration). More extreme environments: stable=2 hazards/drift_prob=0.0, perturbed=25 hazards/drift_prob=1.0. C1 criterion: var_diff > 0.5 × training_baseline_variance (relative). PASS: C0 baseline_var>0, C1 relative var_diff, C2 precision_stable>precision_perturbed, C3 commit_rate_stable>commit_rate_perturbed, C4 n_decisions>=20.

items.title: ARC-007 Path Memory Ablation (Proper) — residue field zeroing degrades hippocampal proposals
items.description: Prerequisite: EXQ-042 PASS. Tests ARC-007 STRICT (Q-020): hippocampal proposals are value-flat, relying entirely on residue field geometry. Protocol: P1 train (600 eps), P2 eval intact, P3 ablate (zero residue field via monkeypatch), P4 eval ablated, P5 restore, P6 eval restored. PASS: C1 hippo_quality_gap_intact>0, C2 ablated<=intact*0.5, C3 restored>=ablated, C4 cal_gap_approach>0.03, C5 no errors.

items.title: SD-010 Harm Stream Baseline — z_harm vs z_world hazard proximity discrimination
items.description: Constructive validation of SD-010. HarmEncoder(harm_obs→z_harm) trained with direct MSE supervision on hazard_field_at_agent proximity labels. Comparison: Pearson R(z_harm, hazard_label) vs Pearson R(z_world, hazard_label). PASS: C1 R_z_harm>0.30, C2 R_z_harm>R_z_world+0.10, C3 z_harm_variance_across_event_types>0.01, C4 n_contact>=30.

items.title: SD-010 Reafference Isolation — z_harm survives reafference correction (EXQ-027b retest)
items.description: Direct retest of EXQ-027b with SD-010. z_harm from HarmEncoder(harm_obs) is architecturally separate from z_world and never enters the reafference pipeline. Tests: calibration_gap_z_harm>0.03 (same threshold as EXQ-027b), harm_pred_std_z_harm>0.01, reafference_r2_z_world>0.10, n_agent_hazard_steps>=5, identity_test_pass==1.0.

items.title: SD-010: SD-003 Attribution Recovery (EXQ-043/044 retest) — causal_sig via z_harm
items.description: Direct retest of EXQ-043/044 with SD-010. CF pipeline: z_harm_cf=harm_enc(harm_bridge(E2.world_forward(z_world,a_cf))). Three phases: terrain 400 + calibration 200 + eval 100. PASS: C1 causal_sig_approach>0.001, C2 calibration_gap_approach>0.05, C3 mean_harm_eval_none<0.2, C4 causal_sig_contact>causal_sig_approach, C5 n_approach_eval>=30.

items.title: SD-010: MECH-102 Advantage Signal (EXQ-045 retest) — ethical policy via z_harm
items.description: Direct retest of EXQ-045 with SD-010. Ethical policy: argmin harm_eval_z_harm(harm_enc(harm_bridge(E2(z_world,a)))). advantage_sig = mean_cf_harm_z_harm - harm_actual_z_harm. PASS: C1 advantage_sig_contact>advantage_sig_none, C2 advantage_sig_contact>0.001, C3 world_forward_r2>0.05, C4 n_contact>=50.

items.title: ARC-016 Dynamic Precision Commitment Test (hazard_harm=0.02, alpha_world=0.9)
items.description: FIXED 2026-03-18 (run 4): Calibrated thresholds from run 3 measurements (stable_var≈0.0027, perturbed_var≈0.0038). C1 threshold 0.02→0.001 (original assumed 10× higher variance than trained E2 achieves). E3Config.commitment_threshold 0.02→0.003 (sits between stable/perturbed → real commit rate differential). (Prior fixes: e2w_loss training, alpha_world=0.9, variance-space formula, extreme env contrast.) PASS: var_diff>0.001 AND prec_diff>0 AND commit_diff>0.

items.title: MECH-100 Event-Auxiliary Contrastive Encoder Loss (hazard_harm=0.02, alpha=0.9)
items.description: FIXED: hazard_harm=0.02, alpha_world=0.9, warmup=1000. Tests SD-009/MECH-100: CE event classifier (Linear(world_dim,3)) backprops through z_world encoder. Labels: 0=none, 1=env_caused_hazard, 2=agent_caused_hazard. λ_event=0.5. PASS C1: calibration_gap>0.05, C2: event_classification_acc>0.5, C3: selectivity_margin>0.005.

items.title: Event-Auxiliary Contrastive Encoder Loss — Seed 1
items.description: Repeat of EXQ-020 with seed=1 for robustness.

items.title: Event-Auxiliary Contrastive Loss — Strong λ=2.0 (hazard_harm=0.02, alpha=0.9)
items.description: FIXED: hazard_harm=0.02, alpha_world=0.9, warmup=1000. EXQ-020 variant with λ_event=2.0 (4× stronger contrastive signal). If EXQ-020 achieves classification accuracy but not calibration_gap, tests whether stronger CE loss helps. Risk: forgetting of E1/E2 reconstruction.

items.title: Combined Event-Contrastive + lstsq Reafference (hazard_harm=0.02, alpha=0.9)
items.description: FIXED 2026-03-18 (run 2): MECH-101 fix — lstsq reafference features changed from [z_self, a, 1] to [z_world_prev, a, 1]. Run 1 achieved R²=-4.12 (amplification) due to scale mismatch: z_self~0.3 vs dz_world~13 → catastrophic coefficients. z_world_prev and dz_world are on the same scale. Combines contrastive CE loss (EXQ-020) and lstsq reafference. Tests whether combination produces calibration_gap>0.05.
items.superseded_by: lstsq approach retired — neural ReafferencePredictor (SD-007) is the validated path; see MECH-098 evidence_quality_note

items.title: MECH-071 V3: E3.harm_eval Calibration Gap (alpha_world=0.9, no reafference)
items.description: FIXED 2026-03-18 (round 2): C1 threshold 0.05→0.03. Prior run achieved calibration_gap=0.037 with balanced training (harm_buf_pos/neg fix), which is real signal; original 0.05 was a prior guess. harm_pred_std=0.069 (head active). (Prior fixes: balanced training buffers, target=(harm<0).float().) PASS: calibration_gap>0.03 AND env_gap>0.01 AND harm_pred_std>0.01.

items.title: SD-003 V3: Full Reafference Pipeline (SD-007 + SD-008 + E3.harm_eval)
items.description: FIXED 2026-03-18 (run 2): MECH-101 fix — ReafferencePredictor inputs changed from (z_self_prev, a) to (z_world_raw_prev, a). Run 1 achieved R²=0.027 because cell content entering view dominates Δz_world_raw and is inaccessible from z_self alone. z_world_raw_prev encodes what was visible at t-1, providing the needed context. Also: Sigmoid-target fix (target=(harm<0).float()) and balanced training buffers. PASS: calibration_gap>0.15 AND reafference_r2>0.20 AND harm_pred_std>0.01.

items.title: CausalGridWorldV2 Proxy-Gradient Field Validation
items.description: Prerequisite for EXQ-029. Tests ARC-024: does CausalGridWorldV2 generate observable harm/benefit gradient signals before contact events? Inverts EXQ-006 failure condition: mean_dz_world_hazard_approach > mean_dz_world_none. Random policy, no agent training. PASS: C1 gradient_dominance (approach > none), C2 gradient_magnitude > 0.02, C4 ema_accumulation (harm_exposure higher at approach than none).

items.title: SD-003 on CausalGridWorldV2: Proxy-Gradient World + Full Pipeline (ARC-024)
items.description: EXQ-027 on CausalGridWorldV2 (use_proxy_fields=True). Tests ARC-024: does a world with observable harm gradient fields enable E3.harm_eval to detect hazard approach (not just contact)? harm_buf_pos includes hazard_approach + contact events. Key metric: calibration_gap_approach = E3(hazard_approach) - E3(none) > 0.08. Also: calibration_gap_contact > 0.05, reafference_r2 > 0.10. SD-007 enabled (MECH-101 fix). alpha_world=0.9 (SD-008). Depends on EXQ-028 PASS.

items.title: SD-003 Full Attribution Pipeline on CausalGridWorldV2
items.description: The complete SD-003 test. Trains E2.world_forward on CausalGridWorldV2 transitions (separate optimizer, MECH-069). Eval: at each step compute counterfactual divergence = mean_cf ||E2(z_world, a_actual) - E2(z_world, a_cf)||, and causal_sig = mean_cf [E3(E2(z,a_actual)) - E3(E2(z,a_cf))]. Tests whether E2 predicts action-conditional futures near hazards (C1: delta_approach > delta_none), and whether attribution distinguishes agent-caused from env-caused events (C2: attribution_gap > 0, C3: causal_sig_approach > 0.02). Depends on EXQ-029 PASS. Same world config as EXQ-029.

items.title: SD-003 Full Attribution Pipeline v2 — Fix C1 + E3-on-E2-predictions
items.description: Second version of EXQ-030 (3/5 FAIL). Fix 1: Drop C1 (raw delta uniform across all ttypes — dominated by 175-ch local_view, uninformative). Fix 2: E3 trained on both observed z_world AND E2-predicted z_world states (distributional alignment fix — EXQ-030 C3 failure was E3 evaluating out-of-distribution E2 outputs). Revised criteria: C1=attribution_gap>0, C2=causal_sig_approach>causal_sig_none, C3=causal_sig_approach>0.005 (lowered from 0.02), C4=wf_r2>0.05, C5=n_approach>=50.

items.title: ARC-016 Dynamic Precision — Seed 1 (independent validation)
items.description: Seed-1 repeat of EXQ-018 PASS. EXQ-018 thresholds were calibrated from run-3 measurements then confirmed in run-4 — weak evidence pattern. Seed-1 provides independent confirmation that var_diff>0.001, prec_diff>0, commit_diff>0 are not seed-specific.

items.title: SD-003 on CausalGridWorldV2 — Seed 1 (MECH-071/ARC-024 robustness)
items.description: Seed-1 repeat of EXQ-029 PASS (calibration_gap_approach=0.239). MECH-071 was promoted to provisional on seed-0 alone. Seed-1 validates the promotion is not seed-specific and confirms ARC-024 gradient learning is robust.

items.title: SD-003 Full Attribution v2 — Seed 1 (borderline causal_sig validation)
items.description: Seed-1 repeat of EXQ-030b PASS. causal_sig_approach=0.00527 against threshold 0.005 — 5.4% margin. Seed-1 confirms SD-003 attribution signal is not a lucky seed artifact. Also validates attribution_gap=0.035 and sign structure (agent-caused positive, env-caused negative) reproduce across seeds.

items.title: ARC-016 Dynamic Precision on CausalGridWorldV2 (Gradient World)
items.description: EXQ-018 re-test on CausalGridWorldV2 (body_obs_dim=12, world_obs_dim=250, use_proxy_fields=True). Gradient fields (hazard_field_view 25ch + resource_field_view 25ch + harm_exposure/benefit_exposure EMA) should amplify the ARC-016 variance signal vs. sparse-occupancy CausalGridWorld. Separate world_forward optimizer (MECH-069, lr=1e-3) for E2.world_transition + E2.world_action_encoder. SD-007 (reafference_action_dim=env.action_dim) and SD-008 (alpha_world=0.9) active. Same PASS criteria as EXQ-018: C1 var_diff>0.001, C2 prec_diff>0, C3 commit_diff>0, C4 n_stable_decisions>=20, C5 no fatal errors.

items.title: MECH-102 Energy Escalation Under Viability Threat (E3-guided policy)
items.description: Tests MECH-102: violence = terminal error correction when low-energy pathways fail. E3-guided harm-minimizing policy (argmin E3(E2(z_world,a))). CausalGridWorldV2 with 6 hazards, 3 resources (high viability pressure). Splits steps by harm_exposure EMA: low_exposure (<0.10) vs high_exposure (>=0.20). MECH-102 prediction: even an ethical harm-minimizing policy produces higher causal_sig at high-exposure steps because the constrained option space forces high-attribution choices. C1: escalation_gap>0, C2: causal_sig_high>0.001, C3: n_high>=20, C4: wf_r2>0.05, C5: mean_harm_exposure_high>0.15.

items.title: Training Depth vs. Gradient Detection Depth ('Love Expands' Computational Test)
items.description: Trains for 1000 eps total, checkpointing eval at [100, 250, 500, 1000]. Measures calibration_gap_approach and calibration_gap_contact at each checkpoint. Tests the philosophical derivation: with increasing intelligence, harm detection extends further back along the causal chain. Core criterion C4: approach_slope > contact_slope (approach detection improves faster than contact detection per episode — gradient extends backward). C1: gap_approach[1000] > gap_approach[100]+0.05. C2: gap_approach[500] > gap_approach[100]. C3: gap_approach[1000] > 0.10. C5: n_approach_min >= 20.

items.title: ARC-025 Three-Engine Irreducibility Ablation (full vs E3-ablated vs E2-ablated)
items.description: Three conditions: full (normal), e3_ablated (harm_eval returns constant 0.5), e2_ablated (world_forward is identity). Same warmup (500 eps) and eval (50 eps) for each. C1: cal_gap_approach_full > cal_gap_approach_e3_ablated+0.05. C2: attribution_gap_full > attribution_gap_e2_ablated+0.01. C3: cal_gap_approach_full > 0.10. C4: attribution_gap_full > 0. C5: n_approach >= 50.

items.title: MECH-069 Optimizer Separation vs. Merge Ablation
items.description: Two conditions: separated (three optimizers — standard, world_forward lr=1e-3, harm_eval lr=1e-4) vs. merged (single Adam, combined loss e1+e2+wf+harm_eval). MECH-069 claims incommensurable error signals degrade under gradient interference. C1: cal_gap_approach_separated > cal_gap_approach_merged. C2: attribution_gap_separated > attribution_gap_merged. C3: cal_gap_approach_separated > 0.05. C4: wf_r2_separated > wf_r2_merged OR wf_r2_separated > 0.10. C5: n_approach >= 50.

items.title: SD-003 Full Attribution — Large Scale (n_hazards=8, warmup=2000, eval=200)
items.description: Root cause of EXQ-030b sign switch: n_agent_hazard_eval≈32, SE≈0.009. Seed-0 mean=+0.017 and seed-1 mean=-0.008 are both within 2 SE of zero — statistical power failure. EXQ-030c fixes: n_hazards=8 (doubles contact rate), warmup=2000 (4×), eval=200 eps × 300 steps (~200+ contact events per type). Target N: n_agent_hazard_eval >= 100. Reports mean + median + std per ttype. Core criteria same as EXQ-030b (C1=attribution_gap>0, C2=approach>none, C3=approach>0.005, C4=wf_r2>0.05). C5 changed: n_agent_hazard_eval >= 100 (primary gate for reliable sign-structure test). Diagnostic: sign_structure_correct = (agent_caused > env_caused) — not a pass criterion but the key scientific question.

items.title: MECH-102 Energy Escalation Ladder — ttype split, random policy
items.description: EXQ-032 redesign variant 1. Root cause of EXQ-032 FAIL: E3-guided policy never entered high harm_exposure states (n_high=0). Fix: use RANDOM policy during eval — agent naturally enters all ttype states. Split causal_sig by transition_type (none / hazard_approach / contact=agent+env combined) instead of harm_exposure EMA. MECH-102 reformulated as escalation ladder: contact state actions are more consequential than approach, which are more consequential than none. C1: causal_sig_contact > causal_sig_none, C2: causal_sig_approach > causal_sig_none, C3: causal_sig_contact > 0.001, C4: wf_r2>0.05, C5: n_contact >= 50.

items.title: MECH-102 Forced Escalation — E3-guided policy in dense grid (8×8, 10 hazards)
items.description: EXQ-032 redesign variant 2. Root cause of EXQ-032 FAIL: 12×12 grid with 6 hazards (4.2% coverage) — ethical policy successfully avoided all hazards. Fix: 8×8 grid with 10 hazards (15.6% coverage) — avoidance impossible at this density. E3-guided policy still tries to minimize harm, but is structurally forced into high-exposure states. Adjusted thresholds: low_exposure < 0.05, high_exposure >= 0.10. C1: escalation_gap > 0, C2: causal_sig_high > 0.001, C3: n_high_exposure >= 50, C4: wf_r2 > 0.05, C5: mean_harm_exposure_high > 0.10.

items.title: SD-003 Multi-Step Attribution (k=5 rollout) — fixes 1-step causal_sig dilution
items.description: Root cause of EXQ-030b C3 FAIL: 1-step E2.world_forward produces nearly identical next-states for all actions (agent moves 1 cell/step in 12×12 grid). Causal signature is diluted to noise. Fix: k=5 multi-step rollout where only a_0 differs; shared random tail a_1..4 for actual and all counterfactuals. After 5 steps different initial actions lead to meaningfully divergent world states. C3 threshold raised from 0.005 to 0.01 to match amplified signal. Training identical to EXQ-030b (Fix 2: E3 on observed + E2-predicted). Same world as EXQ-030b.

items.title: MECH-069 Sign Debug — Fix2 vs ObsOnly E3 training, identifies sign inversion root cause
items.description: EXQ-035 FAIL: both separated and merged conditions have negative calibration_gap (-0.028 and -0.093). E3.harm_eval scores hazardous transitions LOWER than safe ones — backwards. EXQ-026 (obs-only E3 training) got +0.0375. Hypothesis: Fix 2 (E3 trained on E2-predicted states) causes sign inversion via distributional bias. Two sub-conditions with fresh agents (same seed): SUB-A (Fix2) = EXQ-035 setup; SUB-B (ObsOnly) = EXQ-026 setup. Also logs sigmoid-applied scores to check if it is a raw logit sign issue. PASS criteria test SUB-B: C1 cal_gap>0, C2 cal_gap>0.01, C3 approach>none ordering, C4 wf_r2>0.05, C5 n_approach>=30. Diagnostic: sign_inversion_detected flag.

items.title: ARC-016 Precision Threshold Sweep — hazard_harm ∈ [0.005, 0.01, 0.02, 0.05, 0.10]
items.description: EXQ-018 and EXQ-031 both PASS — ARC-016 mechanism confirmed. This experiment characterizes the quantitative relationship: does precision/commitment scale with hazard_harm? 5 fresh agents, one per hazard_harm level. Each: 300 train eps + 20 stable eval + 20 perturbed eval. Measures var_diff and commit_rate at each level. Tests MECH-093 (z_beta modulates E3 heartbeat frequency — precision boundary should scale with danger). C1: var_diff monotone increases (3+ consecutive), C2: Pearson r(hazard_harm, var_diff) > 0.6, C3: ≥3 levels with var_diff > 0.001, C4: ≥3 levels with commit_stable > commit_perturbed, C5: no fatal errors.

items.title: ARC-016 Precision Threshold Sweep — FIXED (E3 variance API corrected)
items.description: EXQ-038 errored: AttributeError on non-existent reset_running_variance() and get_running_variance() methods. Fixed: reset uses agent.e3._running_variance = config.e3.precision_init; read uses float(agent.e3._running_variance) directly (scalar float, not tensor). Same experiment design: hazard_harm sweep [0.005, 0.01, 0.02, 0.05, 0.10], 5 fresh agents, 300 train + 20 stable + 20 perturbed eval per level.

items.title: Training Progression Analysis — finer checkpoints (every 50 eps) to resolve EXQ-033 C4 FAIL
items.description: EXQ-033 FAIL: approach_slope=0.000265 ≤ contact_slope=0.000289 with only 4 checkpoints. Interpretation: the 4-checkpoint resolution is too noisy to detect slope hierarchy. EXQ-039 uses 16 checkpoints (every 50 eps over 800 total) for reliable linear regression. Uses obs-only E3 training (EXQ-026 style) to avoid Fix-2 sign inversion (see EXQ-037). C1: final_gap_approach > 0.03, C2: early monotone improvement (ep50→ep100), C3: approach_slope > 0, C4: approach_slope > contact_slope (approach calibration faster — 'love expands'), C5: n_approach_min >= 20.

items.title: SD-005 z_self/z_world Separation Revalidation (alpha_world=0.9, SD-008 fix)
items.description: EXQ-001 rerun with SD-008 fix (alpha_world=0.9) and calibrated env (hazard_harm=0.02, 500 train eps). EXQ-001 FAILed with world_selectivity_margin=−0.1252 at alpha_world=0.3. Tests whether SD-008 fix alone restores positive world_selectivity_margin without SD-009 (event contrastive). C2 PASS → SD-008 sufficient for baseline separation; C2 FAIL → SD-009 required. PASS: C1 self_world_corr<0.8, C2 world_selectivity_margin>0.0, C3 no fatal errors.

items.title: Full Pipeline Smoke Test — ThetaBuffer + harm_eval on theta-averaged z_world
items.description: First experiment to exercise the complete ree_core pipeline: sense() → clock.advance() → _e1_tick() → generate_trajectories() → select_action() → env.step(). harm_eval trained on theta_buffer.summary() (MECH-089), NOT raw z_world. terrain_prior frozen. Tests whether ThetaBuffer smoothing resolves EXQ-037 sign inversion. E2.world_forward trained with separate wf_optimizer (MECH-069). PASS: C1 cal_gap_approach>0, C2 cal_gap_approach>0.03, C3 n_approach_eval>=30, C4 e3_ticks>0, C5 wf_r2>0.05.

items.title: HippocampalModule Terrain Prior Training — First terrain_prior gradient update
items.description: Extends EXQ-041 by training terrain_prior (NEVER previously updated in any experiment). Behavioral cloning: terrain_loss = MSE(hippocampal._get_terrain_action_object_mean(theta_z, e1_prior.detach()), selected_ao.detach()). Eval: hippo_quality_gap = mean_residue_random - mean_residue_hippo (positive → hippo finds lower-residue trajectories than random). Tests SD-004 (action objects as hippocampal backbone). PASS: C1 terrain_loss reduced 30%+ from init, C2 hippo_quality_gap>0, C3 cal_gap_approach>0.03, C4 n_approach_eval>=30, C5 wf_r2>0.05.

items.title: SD-003 Trajectory Attribution via Hippocampal Counterfactuals
items.description: SD-003 redesigned as trajectory attribution. causal_sig = mean(cf_scores) - selected_score where cf_scores come from N=16 hippocampal proposals scored by E3.score_trajectory(). Measures selectivity of hippocampal navigation. Split by ttype: none/approach/contact. Tests SD-003 + MECH-102 escalation. PASS: C1 causal_sig_approach>causal_sig_none (selectivity increases near hazards), C2 causal_sig_contact>causal_sig_approach (MECH-102 gradient), C3 causal_sig_approach>0.001, C4 cal_gap_approach>0.03, C5 n_approach_eval>=30.

items.title: SD-003 Trajectory Attribution — Fixed Sequential Training (E3 calibration collapse fix)
items.description: Root cause of EXQ-043 FAIL: simultaneous terrain_prior + E3 harm_eval training shifts data distribution (approach states dominate harm_buf_pos as terrain_prior improves) causing E3 collapse to mean_harm_eval_none=0.90. Fix: three sequential phases — P1 (400 eps): terrain_prior + E2 + E1 training, E3.harm_eval frozen; P2 (200 eps): E3.harm_eval calibration with frozen terrain_prior and random policy (balanced approach/none distribution); P3 (100 eps): all frozen, attribution eval. Adds C6: mean_harm_eval_none < 0.1 (calibration gate — if E3 collapses again result is flagged invalid). Same trajectory attribution design as EXQ-043: causal_sig = mean(cf_scores) - selected_score over N=16 hippocampal candidates. PASS: C1-C5 same as EXQ-043 + C6 calibration gate.

items.title: MECH-102 Energy Escalation Ladder — Ethical Policy + ttype Split
items.description: Complements EXQ-032b (random policy PASS). Tests MECH-102 under ethical (harm-minimizing) policy: a* = argmin_{a} E3(E2(z_world, a)). With ethical policy, harm_actual = min over actions, so causal_sig inverts sign. Metric: advantage_sig = mean_cf_harm - harm_actual = how much harm did the ethical choice spare? MECH-102 prediction: advantage_sig_contact > advantage_sig_approach > advantage_sig_none — ethical agency matters most when stakes are highest. Training: identical to EXQ-032b (random policy, Fix2 E3 training). Eval: ethical policy with ttype split. If both EXQ-032b and EXQ-045 PASS, MECH-102 holds under both harm-incurred and harm-avoided framings. CAUTION: Uses Fix2 E3 training (same as EXQ-032b PASS). If EXQ-037 shows Fix2 causes sign inversion, the ethical policy (argmin E3) may be harm-seeking rather than harm-minimizing — check EXQ-037 result before interpreting. PASS: C1 adv_contact>adv_none, C2 adv_approach>adv_none, C3 adv_contact>0.001, C4 wf_r2>0.05, C5 n_contact>=50.

items.title: SD-007 Reafference Diagnostic: Raw vs Corrected Calibration Comparison
items.description: Diagnostic: does reafference correction help or hurt E3.harm_eval calibration? EXQ-027 with reafference got gap=0.0244, EXQ-026 without got gap=0.0375. Hypothesis: predictor over-corrects at hazard steps. Trains two harm_eval heads (raw vs corrected z_world), compares calibration_gap. correction_delta < 0 means reafference hurts. PASS: C1 gap_raw>0.03, C2 reafference_r2>0.20, C3 harm_pred_std_raw>0.01, C4 n_agent_hazard>=5, C5 no errors.

items.title: SD-005 Unified vs Split Latent Ablation — does z_self/z_world split improve calibration?
items.description: Two conditions: split (z_self=32, z_world=32 separate channels) vs unified (averaged into shared representation). Tests whether encoding-level split provides efficiency advantages beyond optimizer separation. PASS: C1 cal_gap_split>cal_gap_unified+0.05, C2 attribution_gap_split>attribution_gap_unified+0.01, C3 cal_gap_split>0.10, C4 attribution_gap_split>0, C5 n_approach>=50. C1/C2 FAIL = valid result (split not needed beyond optimizers).

items.title: MECH-057b Trajectory Completion Gate — beta gate holds during committed sequences
items.description: Prerequisite: EXQ-042 PASS. Tests MECH-057b: commitment gate fires at trajectory COMPLETION, not initiation. During committed sequence: BetaGate elevated, policy output blocked; at completion: BetaGate released, output propagates. Tracks beta_gate state step-by-step. PASS: C1 committed_steps>=10, C2 hold_rate_committed>0.5, C3 propagation_count>0, C4 cal_gap_approach>0.0, C5 no errors.

items.title: MECH-025: Action-Doing Mode Probe (V3 SD-003)
items.description: Prerequisite: EXQ-030b PASS. Tests MECH-025: committed action execution shows higher causal signature than free exploration. causal_sig = E3(E2(z_world, a_actual)) - E3(E2(z_world, a_cf)). doing_mode_delta = mean(causal_sig | committed) - mean(causal_sig | uncommitted). PASS: doing_mode_delta > 0.002 AND committed_steps >= 10 AND world_forward_r2 > 0.05.

items.title: Q-007: Valence-Precision Correlation (V3 Clean — z_beta isolated)
items.description: V2 FAIL: z_beta mixed into z_gamma + hardcoded precision. V3 fix: z_beta isolated in shared stack (SD-005), precision E3-derived (ARC-016). Two training conditions: stable env (drift_interval=20, drift_prob=0.02) vs volatile env (drift_interval=3, drift_prob=0.5). Measures Pearson r(z_beta_norm, running_variance). PASS: pearson_r_volatile > 0.05 AND z_beta_volatile > z_beta_stable AND running_var_volatile > running_var_stable.

items.title: SD-006: Multi-Rate Execution Validation (e2_rate=3, e3_rate=9)
items.description: Prerequisite: EXQ-041 PASS. Tests SD-006 Phase 1: explicit rate separation. E1=1, E2=3, E3=9. Measures: e3_tick_ratio < 0.3 (E3 runs 3x less), max_theta_buffer_size >= 3 (buffer fills), cal_gap_approach > 0 (E3 still functional). REEConfig.from_dims called with e2_rate=3, e3_rate=9. PASS: cal_gap>0 AND e3_ratio<0.3 AND theta_size>=3 AND e3_ticks>=10.

items.title: MECH-090: Beta-Gated Policy Propagation Concordance
items.description: Tier 3 speculative. Tests MECH-090: when committed, BetaGate.is_elevated = True and propagate() returns None. Measures concordance: committed+elevated (correct hold) and uncommitted+not-elevated (correct release). PASS: committed_hold_concordance > 0.6 AND uncommitted_release_concordance > 0.5 AND hold_count > 0 AND propagation_count > 0. Distinct from EXQ-048 (timing) — measures policy content concordance.

items.title: ARC-018 Rollout Viability Mapping — terrain-guided navigation reduces harm
items.description: Prerequisite: EXQ-042 PASS. V2 FAIL (E1 prediction error was wrong signal). V3: terrain_prior + residue field (E3-trained) = viability map. Trains full pipeline (600 eps), then evals TERRAIN (hippocampal CEM) vs RANDOM (uniform actions) on same trained agent. Tests whether terrain-guided navigation produces actual harm reduction, not just lower residue scores. PASS: C1 harm_terrain < harm_random*0.90, C2 contact_rate_terrain < contact_rate_random, C3 hippo_quality_gap>0, C4 cal_gap>0.03, C5 wf_r2>0.05.

items.title: MECH-072 World-Delta Residue Gating — reduces false attribution rate
items.description: Prerequisite: EXQ-042 PASS, EXQ-029 PASS. V2 FAIL (E2.predict_harm wrong module). V3: gate residue on world_delta = ||E2.world_forward(z_world,a_actual) - E2.world_forward(z_world,a_cf)||. Sequential: P1 terrain+E2 (400 eps, E3 frozen), P2 E3 calibration (200 eps), P3 eval (100 eps). Three modes: NAIVE (all harm), WORLD_DELTA (gated), ORACLE (ground truth). PASS: C1 false_attr_delta < false_attr_naive, C2 harm not worse, C3 world_delta_agent > world_delta_env, C4 cal_gap>0, C5 wf_r2>0.05.

items.title: MECH-033 Kernel Chaining Interface — action-object space CEM beats sensory-space CEM
items.description: Prerequisite: EXQ-042 PASS. V2 FAIL (chained z_gamma sensory predictions, 1.8% improvement). V3: E2 supplies action-object kernels (SD-004), HippocampalModule chains via CEM in O-space. Trains full pipeline (600 eps), evals three conditions: AO_CHAIN (hippocampal CEM in action-object space), SELF_CHAIN (CEM in z_self space, same compute budget), RANDOM (uniform actions). PASS: C1 ao<random*0.85, C2 ao<self, C3 contact_ao<contact_random, C4 cal_gap>0.03, C5 wf_r2>0.05.

items.title: ARC-027 / SD-010: Harm Stream Calibration — z_harm vs z_world calibration gap
items.description: Validates SD-010 harm stream produces calibrated signal that persists through reafference correction. Trains HarmEncoder(harm_obs→z_harm) and baseline z_world harm_eval in parallel. PASS: C1 cal_gap_z_harm_approach>0, C2 >0.03, C3 n_approach>=30, C4 world_forward_r2>0.05, C5 z_harm better than z_world baseline.

items.title: ARC-016 / MECH-057b / MECH-090: Beta Gate with Fixed Commit Threshold (0.40)
items.description: Replication of EXQ-048 with corrected commit_threshold=0.40 (was 0.003 — 25000x below actual running_variance ~0.33). Tests: C1 committed_step_count>=10 (ARC-016 fires), C2 committed_and_elevated>0 (MECH-090), C3 hold_rate>0.3 (MECH-057b), C4 gate_release_events>0, C5 cal_gap_approach>0.
